element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Each dinosaur has unique ability; players learn through discovery of card suits (hearts, spades, clovers, diamonds)
Ice prevents sliding when riding blue camptosaurus; teaches environmental puzzle-solving
Red camptosaurus can breathe fire and swim over lava to certain point; spatial navigation challenge
Elasmosaurus is only dinosaur that survives underwater; encourages preparation via inventory system
Changes spatial grammar from horizontal; tests aerial navigation with pteranodon or swimming
If defeated, boss relocates; player must clear additional stage to rematch
Different audio cue signals hidden egg location; keys unlock bonus zones or shortcuts
Accumulate in inventory; no longer upgrade to fireballs like original game
Speed mechanic; cannot carry to next stage (unlike original); completion bonus triggers egg selection

typical_relief_triggers: Discovering a dinosaur companion via hidden egg
Finding shortcut key in bonus zone
Egg selection spin reward after stage clear
typical_tension_triggers: Entering hazard terrain (lava, ice, underwater) without required dinosaur
Boss relocation after defeat (forces additional stage clear)
Lack of checkpoints within stage (full restart on death)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Bite-sized stages with exploration pressure; tension builds through environmental hazards requiring specific dinosaurs; relief comes from discovery rewards (hidden eggs, shortcuts); eight islands provide episodic structure

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Hidden eggs marked by distinct audio cue when struck; optional shortcuts and item rooms accessed via key collection
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Striking specific locations triggers different noise indicating hidden egg presence; encourages systematic exploration

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduces basic platforming and first dinosaur companions; ice terrain teaches environmental adaptation
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Lava and underwater stages demand specific dinosaur preparation; vertical-scrolling sections increase spatial complexity
difficulty_curve.late_game: Boss relocation mechanic forces extended level sequences; combination of hazards requires inventory strategy
notable_spikes: Island boss encounters (especially when relocated after defeat)
Vertical-scrolling sections requiring pteranodon or precise swimming

notable_level_patterns.description: Environmental hazards (ice, lava, underwater) are introduced in stages where players can discover the required dinosaur before or immediately encounter the terrain. Encourages hidden egg exploration.
Defeating a boss moves it to another stage on the same island. Player must complete current stage plus additional stage to rematch. Extends engagement and punishes boss-only advancement attempts.
Keys hidden in eggs can transport player to item rooms or skip to next island. Rewards exploration and provides difficulty scaling for replays or speedrunning.
Mix of horizontal and vertical stages breaks monotony and changes required dinosaur abilities; vertical sections emphasize flight (pteranodon) or swimming (elasmosaurus).
Before each stage, player must choose which dinosaur and how many hammers to bring. Creates strategic friction: preparing for unknown hazards ahead.
